title: Official Launch Run
description: Swipe a launch marshal skiff across the crimson runway to dodge incoming launch debris and ride official clearance beams for huge score chains.

howToPlayJson.title: Official Launch Run
howToPlayJson.objective: Survive a 3-minute official launch countdown in the Crimson Desert by dodging debris, flares, and collapsing scaffold pieces while choosing when to enter flashing clearance lanes that boost score but increase danger.

controls.outcome: The skiff snaps between runway lanes to avoid hazards.

controls.input: Keep your finger down while staying in the marked lane
controls.outcome: Builds the Official multiplier as long as you remain inside the beam without getting hit.

controls.input: Swipe out of the marked lane at any time
controls.outcome: Banks the current multiplier before the beam expires or turns risky.

steps: Start on a three-lane desert launch runway with a 180-second countdown.
Debris streams in from the top: fuel drums, banner poles, flare rockets, and falling launch parts.
Every few seconds, HQ marks one lane with an Official Clearance beam.
Staying inside that beam charges a score multiplier, but the next hazard wave increasingly targets or crosses that same lane.
Swipe out early to keep safe and bank the multiplier, or stay longer for bigger points before the beam collapses.
Hazard speed, cross-lane patterns, and fake clearance feints intensify until the final launch burst.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Earn points for every second survived and every hazard wave cleared. Staying in an Official Clearance beam increases a multiplier up to x8, and swiping out before impact banks it into your score. Getting hit resets the
tips: Do not sit in a clearance beam too long; the safest beam is rarely the highest-value beam.
Bank multipliers before mixed cross-lane waves, especially when flare sirens sound.
Audio helps: a clean chime means safe beam start, a harsher alarm means that lane is about to be punished.
Short, early banks usually beat one greedy run if you want consistent leaderboard scores.
